scenario: An Iris fintech architect onboards 12 new modelers to Camunda 8.8 DMN tables that evaluate 22000 transactions per day. The architect wants the modelers to know how many input columns a single decision table can declare so they can plan for tables with both 1 and 5 input columns.
question: How many input clauses can a Camunda 8 DMN decision table declare, according to the decision-table-input docs?

options.text: A Camunda 8 DMN decision table can declare exactly one input clause, with all additional input data passed through a child inputExpression element on that input element
A Camunda 8 DMN decision table can declare two input clauses at most, with the second input clause acting as a fallback input for the rules in the decision table
A Camunda 8 DMN decision table can declare one or more input clauses, each represented by an input XML element inside the decisionTable XML element of the deployed DMN file
A Camunda 8 DMN decision table can declare input clauses only when the hit policy is COLLECT, with each input clause represented by an input XML element inside the decisionTable element

a.text: Incorrect. The docs explicitly say a decision table can have one or more input clauses, so the count is not restricted to exactly one input clause per decision table in the deployed DMN file.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs do not cap the number of input clauses at two, nor do they describe any fallback role for a second input; they allow one or more input clauses on the decision table.

c.text: Correct. The decision-table-input docs state that a decision table can have one or more inputs, also called input clauses, and that each input clause is represented by an input XML element inside the decisionTable element.

d.text: Incorrect. Hit policy and input count are independent in the docs; a decision table can have one or more input clauses regardless of whether the hit policy is COLLECT, UNIQUE, ANY, FIRST, or RULE ORDER.

explanation: The decision-table-input docs say a decision table can have one or more inputs, also called input clauses. Each input clause is represented by an input XML element nested inside the decisionTable XML element of the deployed DMN file.
